name: Island formula / quantum extremal surfaces
summary: Recent semi-classical gravity calculations show that the entanglement entropy of Hawking radiation follows the unitary Page curve when one includes 'islands' — disconnected regions inside the black hole that the radiation knows about via quantum extremal surface prescriptions. Replica wormholes in the gravitational path integral provide the underlying microscopic mechanism. Reproduces the Page curve in JT gravity coupled to matter without invoking firewalls or new principles.

addresses_puzzles.note: Within the controlled setting (JT gravity, asymptotically-AdS, semiclassical bulk), the information paradox is resolved: the Page curve emerges from the gravitational path integral, the radiation reconstructs the interior at late times via entanglement-wedge reconstruction. Generalisation to physical 4D evaporating black holes is in active development.

Quantum-extremal-surface + entanglement-wedge prescription yields a unitary Page curve for black-hole evaporation purely from semi-classical gravity, providing a concrete answer to the BH information paradox (Penington 2020; Almheiri-Engelhardt-Marolf-Maxfield 2020).

introduces.note: Builds on existing gravity + QFT framework; the 'island' / QES prescription is a derivation, not a new postulate. Compression score is essentially infinite — closes a major puzzle with no new free parameters.

history: Penington (2019), 'Entanglement Wedge Reconstruction and the Information Paradox', JHEP 09, 002 (2020), arXiv:1905.08255. Almheiri, Engelhardt, Marolf, Maxfield (2019), 'The entropy of bulk quantum fields and the entanglement wedge of an evaporating black hole', JHEP 12, 063, arXiv:1905.08762. Replica-wormhole derivation: Almheiri, Hartman, Maldacena, Shaghoulian, Tajdini (2019, arXiv:1911.12333) and Penington, Shenker, Stanford, Yang (2019, arXiv:1911.11977). One of the most active areas in quantum gravity 2019-present.

references.citation: Penington, 'Entanglement Wedge Reconstruction and the Information Paradox', JHEP 09, 002 (2020)
Almheiri, Engelhardt, Marolf, Maxfield, 'The entropy of bulk quantum fields and the entanglement wedge of an evaporating black hole', JHEP 12, 063 (2019)
